name: Mirrored
summary: Mirror-uploader service that uploads files to multiple downstream hosts and returns an aggregate download page.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

developer.notes: Mirrored publishes official HTTPS API documentation for uploads, account info, and file information.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types depend on selected downstream hosts; the current homepage lists regular file hosts and a separate group of video-only hosts.

security.notes: The live product and API documentation are served over HTTPS. Mirrored does not position itself as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: Mirrored homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verified in a live browser: active local-file and remote-URL upload forms, 750 MB regular upload limit, multiple host choices, and current free service messaging.

sources.label: Mirrored FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering anonymous uploads, non-hosting behavior, and mirror-service workflow.

sources.label: Mirrored API docs
sources.notes: Official API documentation for authenticated HTTPS uploads and file/account info.

max_file_size.notes: The current official homepage says regular users can upload up to 750 MB per file, while PRO users can upload up to 3 GB per file.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

remote_import.notes: The current official upload page exposes a Remote URL Upload mode.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party pages expose local-file upload and remote URL upload modes, but do not publish a torrent or magnet import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Official FAQ says Mirrored is not a file-hosting site; mirrored files live on downstream hosts and Mirrored keeps aggregate links.

retention_model.notes: Conditional: Mirrored stores aggregate links and background-mirrors files to downstream hosts, so long-term availability depends on both Mirrored's link database and the selected mirror hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Mirrored availability and download behavior depend on the selected downstream hosts and their own rules.

name: MultiUp
summary: Mirror-uploader service that can upload a file once and fan it out to multiple downstream file hosts.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are available from the homepage. Free accounts add queue, file-host selection, and torrent controls, while premium removes ads and expands limits.

developer.notes: MultiUp publishes official API upload docs and an official shell-script upload guide for Linux, making the service automation-friendly even though the workflow is mirror-centric.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types and delivery behavior ultimately vary by the downstream hosts selected for mirroring.

security.notes: The public pages do not claim end-to-end encryption for mirrored uploads.

sources.label: MultiUp homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing uploads to multiple file-hosts, plus upload-by-computer, link, torrent, software, and API flows.

sources.label: MultiUp login and plans page
sources.notes: Official account page describing free and premium account capabilities, including host-selection, queue-size, and torrent-related limits.

sources.label: MultiUp API upload docs
sources.notes: Official API documentation for login, fastest-server lookup, file upload, remote upload, torrent workflows, and link checking.

sources.label: MultiUp upload from softwares page
sources.notes: Official page with a shell upload script for Linux, showing first-party CLI-oriented automation support.

max_file_size.notes: Conditional: limits vary by the selected downstream hosts and upload method. The official homepage, FAQ, and API docs advertise computer, link, torrent, software, and API uploads but do not publish one universal cap.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are available from the homepage. Free accounts add queue, file-host selection, and torrent controls, while premium removes ads and expands limits.

remote_import.notes: Official pages and API docs describe upload-by-link / remote upload workflows.

torrent_import.notes: Official pages and API docs describe torrent-based mirroring workflows.

stores_files_itself.notes: Persistent storage varies by downstream hosts rather than by a standalone MultiUp storage bucket.

retention_model.notes: Retention varies by the downstream file hosts used for mirroring.

downstream_dependency.notes: Availability and download behavior ultimately depend on the downstream hosts chosen for mirroring.

name: PolyUploader
summary: Open-source Windows/Tauri mirror-uploader app that uploads local files or remote URLs to 130+ downstream hosts and can create bundled share links.

account.benefits: No PolyUploader account is required to use the app. Downstream hosts may still require accounts or API keys for specific host integrations.

developer.notes: PolyUploader's backend API is open source and powers bundle links, telemetry, and redirects, but the reviewed first-party pages do not present it as a public upload API or CLI for end users. The supported workflow is the Windows GUI app or building the Tauri app from source.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed and blocked formats depend on the selected downstream hosts. The v1.7.0 release notes also mention a prohibited-file-format filter for multi-upload mode.

public_sharing.notes: PolyUploader can generate a single sharing link that bundles multiple host links, and the open-source backend API powers those shareable bundle pages.

security.notes: The official docs, repository, release, and backend API surfaces are served over HTTPS, but PolyUploader does not claim end-to-end encryption for uploaded files. The API repo says telemetry stores host names and upload dates, not full upload links.

sources.label: PolyUploader documentation
sources.notes: Official documentation homepage showing the current 1.7.1 version, free/open-source positioning, uploads from local storage or URL to 130 hosts, API key integration, upload history, profiles, and no-account use.

sources.label: PolyUploader GitHub repository
sources.notes: Official GPL-3.0 repository README describing local/URL upload to 133 hosts, API key support for compatible hosts, expiration/deletion history, upload profiles, single bundle links, no-account use, Rust/Tauri backend, and Windows release/build instructions.

sources.label: PolyUploader releases
sources.notes: Official release page showing latest v1.7.1 and Windows installer availability; v1.7.0 notes mention API-key improvements, telemetry, host support changes, and prohibited-format filtering.

sources.label: PolyUploader API repository
sources.notes: Official backend API repository explaining anonymous statistics/telemetry, unique shareable links bundling multiple upload links, and utility endpoints used by the platform.

sources.label: PolyUploader web demo shutdown
sources.notes: Official notice that the web demonstration is no longer available; users are directed to the Windows software, and the former web demo had a Vercel transfer limit of 5 MB.

max_file_size.notes: Conditional: PolyUploader does not impose one universal file-size cap in the reviewed docs. Its README footnote says file size is restricted by the selected host rather than by the software.

guest_uploads.notes: The official documentation and README both describe no-account use for the app, while some downstream hosts can still require or benefit from API keys.

remote_import.notes: Official docs and README say PolyUploader can upload from local storage or via URL to 130+ hosts.

torrent_import.notes: The reviewed first-party docs and README describe local-file and URL upload workflows, but no torrent or magnet import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: PolyUploader is an uploader and bundle-link tool. Files are uploaded to downstream hosts, while its backend API stores shareable bundle pages and telemetry rather than acting as the final file host.

retention_model.notes: PolyUploader can track upload expiration status and expose deletion controls, but final retention depends on the selected downstream hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: The developer notes say host support depends on a mix of official APIs, cURL-to-JavaScript conversion, and frontend request emulation, so long-term delivery depends on selected host behavior and continued adapter maintenance.

name: UploadOnAll
summary: Mirror-uploader service that uploads one file to multiple downstream file-hosting mirrors, with a current 300 MB max file size and up to 10 selected mirrors.

account.benefits: The visible upload form supports guest use and asks users to choose downstream mirrors before uploading.

developer.notes: The cited first-party page does not document a public API or CLI workflow.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file behavior depends on the selected downstream mirrors.

security.notes: The current page is served over HTTPS, but UploadOnAll does not position itself as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: UploadOnAll homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage says maximum file size is 300 MB and users can choose up to 10 mirrors for free file upload.

sources.label: Rentry limited file-host list
sources.notes: Seed list claimed 300 MB for UploadOnAll; official page confirms 300 MB. It was classified as a mirror uploader rather than a direct host.

max_file_size.notes: The official homepage says maximum file size is 300 MB.

guest_uploads.notes: The visible upload form supports guest use.

remote_import.notes: The current first-party page reviewed here only exposes local-file fan-out, not remote URL import.

torrent_import.notes: The current first-party page reviewed here does not expose torrent import.

stores_files_itself.notes: UploadOnAll is a mirror uploader: it sends the file to selected downstream hosts rather than acting as the final file host.

retention_model.notes: Retention depends on the selected downstream file hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Availability and download behavior depend on the selected mirror hosts, several of which are older/dead-brand hosts in the current page list.

name: Z-o-o-m
summary: Freeware Windows File & Image Uploader desktop app that uploads local files/folders to many downstream file and image hosts, supports host account settings, drag-and-drop queues, scheduler, speed limiter, proxy support, and Wine on Linux.

account.benefits: The program is freeware for common users; downstream host accounts can be configured per host, and a registered/donor version unlocks extra benefits.

developer.notes: Z-o-o-m is a GUI desktop uploader. The cited first-party pages do not publish a public API or command-line workflow for automating Z-o-o-m itself.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types depend on the selected downstream host. The program is positioned for both file and image uploads and supports choosing multiple hosts.

public_sharing.notes: The app uploads to downstream hosts and returns/copies links; the help page documents link/history tabs and link-copy formatting.

security.notes: The website is HTTPS. The privacy policy says account cookies/tokens can be stored locally and protected with AES-256 when the user sets a password, but downstream host storage security depends on each selected service.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verifies File & Image Uploader positioning, newest version 8.5.9 updated 2026-04-25, supported-host update history, and ongoing host/account support.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m download page
sources.notes: Official download page verifies the Windows executable, speed limiter, SOCKS/HTTP proxy support, adapter/IP binding, and Wine compatibility on Linux.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m help page
sources.notes: Official help page verifies upload queues, adding files/folders, drag-and-drop, starting/stopping/re-uploading, selecting one or multiple hosts, host account settings, scheduler, and link-copy formatting.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ verifies freeware status for common users, 10 GB/day freeware upload cap, up to 8 simultaneous files, registered-version benefits, and support/reporting workflow.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m privacy policy
sources.notes: Official privacy policy verifies anonymous website/software use, local storage of downstream host login cookies/tokens, optional AES-256 protection for saved settings, and Google API scopes for YouTube, Google Photos, and Google Drive uploads.

max_file_size.notes: Z-o-o-m does not publish one universal per-file cap because selected downstream hosts determine file-size limits. The FAQ instead publishes a 10 GB/day freeware upload cap and up to 8 simultaneous files.

guest_uploads.notes: The program is freeware for common users and can upload through supported host modules; downstream services may still require accounts for specific hosts or features.

remote_import.notes: The cited help and download pages describe local file/folder upload queues and host account settings, but do not publish a remote URL import workflow.

torrent_import.notes: The cited first-party pages do not publish a torrent or magnet import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Z-o-o-m is uploader software; files are stored by the selected downstream hosts, not by Z-o-o-m as a storage provider.

retention_model.notes: Retention depends on the selected downstream file/image host and any account used there; Z-o-o-m itself stores links/history/settings locally rather than hosting the files.

downstream_dependency.value: Selected downstream hosts determine upload, retention, and download behavior
downstream_dependency.notes: The uploader's behavior depends on the selected host modules, downstream host limits, and configured account credentials.
